biography: Joakim, whose home club is not officially noted within the tournament records, contributed to the Hector Trophée's early competitive landscape through his participation in a single event. This brief yet earnest engagement in the unique pair competition adds a distinct thread to its unfolding narrative.

Throughout his solitary tournament experience, Joakim displayed a committed approach to the game. Despite his diligent efforts, neither the coveted Hector Trophée for the winning pair nor the individual Victor Trophy were secured. His presence on the course was characterized by an earnest application of skill.

Joakim has since concluded his active involvement in Hector events and will, therefore, not be among the field for the Hector Trophée 2026. His participation, though limited, nonetheless occupies a place within the tournament's historical tapestry, illustrating the varied journeys of those who have competed.
